Perplexity for a test corpus with words \( w_1, w_2, \ldots, w_n \) is mathematically defined as \( P(w_1, w_2, \ldots, w_n)^{-1/n} \), where \( ppl(S) = 2^x \) and \( x = -\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \log_2 P(w_i|w_1 \ldots w_{i-1}) \).

The condition for the weights in the linear interpolation equation is that the sum of the weights \( \lambda_i \) must equal 1.
The formula for the max likelihood estimate for bigrams before smoothing is \( P(w_i|w_{i-1}) = \frac{C(w_{i-1}, w_i)}{C(w_{i-1})} \).
The missing probability mass is calculated as \( \alpha(wi−1) = 1 − \frac{\sum_{w} \text{Count}^* (wi−1, w)}{\text{Count}(wi−1)} \).

Cosine similarity is mathematically expressed as:

\[ \cos(u, v) = \frac{u \cdot v}{\|u\|\|v\|} \]

where \( u \cdot v \) is the dot product of vectors \( u \) and \( v \), and \(\|u\|\) and \(\|v\|\) are the magnitudes (or norms) of vectors \( u \) and \( v \), respectively.
The probability notation used for a sequence of words is \( P(w_1, w_2, w_3, \ldots, w_n) \).

The probability expression for \( y = 1 \) given \( t \) and \( c \) is represented as:

\[ P(w_{t+j} | w_t) = \frac{\exp(u_{w_t} \cdot v_{w_{t+j}})}{\sum_{k \in V} \exp(u_{w_t} \cdot v_k)} \]
The perplexity of the GPT-3 model as shown in the slide is 20.5.

The formula for cos(u, v) represents the cosine similarity, which is a common similarity metric that measures the cosine of the angle between two vectors. The larger the cosine similarity, the more similar the two vectors are.
